description: Structurally align horse heart cytochrome c (PDB: 1HRC) with yeast iso-1-cytochrome c (PDB: 1YCC) to analyze their evolutionary conservation. Both structures have been pre-downloaded to ~/PyMOL_Data/structures/. Perform a structural superposition, note the RMSD, and color the two structures distinctly to highlight conserved regions. Identify the highly conserved heme group and its axial ligands (His18 and Met80 in both species). Render a high-resolution PNG figure showing the superimposed structures and save it to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/cytc_conservation.png. Finally, write a plain-text report to ~/PyMOL_Data/cytc_conservation_report.txt that includes the RMSD, mentions both PDB IDs, identifies at least one of the conserved heme-coordinating residues (His18 or Met80), and provides a brief analysis (minimum 5 lines).
